title: German Apple Pudding
description: A rich and creamy French dessert inspired by traditional German apple flavors.
instructions: Peel and slice the apples into thin wedges.
In a bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Add butter and use a pastry blender or fingers to work it into the dry ingredients until crumbly.
Press the mixture evenly into a 9x9-inch baking dish.
Arrange the apple slices on top of the crust, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the apples are tender.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes.
Prepare the pudding according to package instructions.
Pour the pudding over the warm apple mixture.
Return to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the pudding is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
